rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Joint Motion: 1. For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (Ccp Section 877.6) And 2. To Confirm Assignment Of Kbs' Claims (Ccp Section 368.5)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 25, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF 75 HOWARD OWNER, LP , A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP Notice Of Motion And Joint Motion: 1. For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (Ccp Section 877.6) And 2. To Confirm Assignment Of Kbs' Claims (Ccp Section 368.5) Hearing Required. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. rulings.Case Title: M.A. KARAH VS. IZZUDDIN AHMED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal And Memorandum Of Points And Authorizations
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 25, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF M.A. KARAH Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal And Memorandum Of Points And Authorizations is GRANTED. Based on the e-mail chain attached to counsel's declaration there appears to have been a miscommunication between the clerks, Plaintiff, and eventually the judge as Plaintiff appears to have intended to dismiss the case "on Monday" at any time of his choosing, and the Court expected the dismissal to have been filed prior to the only relevant event, the trial call. In order to avoid any such miscommunications in the future, no further e-mails, letter or phone notifications will be accepted by the Court regarding the trial call calendar from anyone. Parties must appear at the trial call unless the dismissal, stipulation for entry of judgment, etc. is on file by the time of the trial call. Plaintiff shall have a copy of the voluntary dismissal at the hearing and present it to the Court for review. Plaintiff's counsel must appear in person to present the proposed dismissal. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1111 JONES, LLC VS. PRESTON MARKS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 25, 2024 line 12. PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant is hereby enjoined from tampering with, covering, obstructing, unplugging, moving, repositioning, removing and destroying security cameras or electrical wires in the hallways and other common areas. Motion is otherwise denied. This is an action for unlawful detainer predicated on a Notice attached to the complaint describing the conduct constituting purported nuisance. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a broad scope preliminary injunction maybe granted in this special proceeding based by definition of the cause of action for unlawful detainer exclusively on the predicate notice. This special proceeding is not the proper forum to address the Plaintiff's other concerns. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE TIDES CENTER VS. JEROME ELLIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 25, 2024 line 14. DEFENDANT JEROME ELLIS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states cause of action for unlawful detainer based on nuisance and termination of the lease. Notice unequivocally terminates the tenancy and requires the tenant to quit by a date certain. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that detailed information regarding the informal meeting must be included in the Notice. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: THE TIDES CENTER VS. DOUGLAS OCHOA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 25, 2024 line 15. DEFENDANT DOUGLAS OCHOA DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Notice to Quit sufficiently and clearly provides Defendant with two grounds for the termination of Defendant's lease. Defendant's demurrer addressed only one of these grounds, violation of the lease. Demurrer to only a part of the sole cause of action for unlawful detainer is not proper. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
